subject: Travelers Claim I9G2378 — Abdul Deef Request for Reconsideration
adjuster: Lauren Cook (Outside Claim Representative, Property — Virginia-Carolina Claim Center, lcook4@travelers.com, 571-596-1933)

messages.sender: Lauren Cook (Travelers Outside Claim Representative, Property)
messages.body: Good Morning, Attached to this email is your settlement letter and copy of your estimate. I reviewed your contractor's photos and estimate and based on what he provided me I am standing by my decision that he is pointing out normal wear and tear to the shingles rather than damage due to hail. Please let me know if you have any questions or concerns. Thank you for your time, Lauren Cook | Outside Claim Representative | Property, Travelers, Virginia-Carolina Claim Center, W: 571.596.1933 F: 877.389.4687. With 24/7 access to your claim online via MyTravelers; you can check your claim status, view your deductible, interact with us and upload documents. Available to Insureds and Contractors - To upload documents to your claim, please use this link: Claim Upload Center.

Hello, As a long-time and loyal customer of Travelers, I am deeply disappointed with your recent decision regarding the claim for my home. I chose Travelers to ensure that my home would be adequately protected in the event of unforeseen circumstances, and I believe your current decision falls short of addressing the full needs of my property following the storm damage. My contractor, who is copied on this email, has provided detailed documentation that explains why the proposed solution is inadequate and outlines the most effective approach to restore my home to its original condition. Specifically, this includes a full roof and siding replacement, which we believe is necessary to ensure the integrity and safety of my home. I kindly request that you review and revise your decision and estimate to reflect a complete roof approval. If you require any further information or clarification, please feel free to contact either myself or my contractor directly. Thank you for your attention to this matter. I look forward to your prompt response. Sincerely, Abdulasis Deef.

denialText: I reviewed your contractor's photos and estimate and based on what he provided me I am standing by my decision that he is pointing out normal wear and tear to the shingles rather than damage due to hail.

he is pointing out normal wear and tear to the shingles rather than damage due to hail
Attached to this email is your settlement letter and copy of your estimate
carrierTactic: Travelers' Lauren Cook reclassifies contractor-documented damage as 'normal wear and tear' to dismiss roof replacement. Standard playbook — re-characterize hail damage as pre-existing condition. Travelers patents emphasize aerial imagery + drone-based damage assessment (top P&C AI patent holder).
rooferTactic: Homeowner-led reconsideration letter (more weight than contractor-led for PA-warning reasons). Emphasizes loyalty + dignity + 'detailed documentation' from contractor. Roof + siding both requested.
patentMapping: Travelers operates the largest insurance drone program (700+ FAA-certified pilots). Their 'wear and tear' classifier is the same engine documented in WO2018156846 (Dolphin AI) and US20190304026 (Betterview) which both define wear-and-tear visual signatures (granular wear contrast, uniform asphalt loss, edge curvature). Lauren Cook is invoking the AI-trained eye, not her own opinion.
